findings.description: Headline/heading and body-text color application rules (Brand Blue/White for headings; Graphite/White for body) are present in the source but have no corresponding rule in the candidate group, leaving a large portion of the blob's normative content uncaptured.

payload.description: Headlines/headings must use Brand Blue (White on dark/gradient backgrounds); body text must use Graphite #212121 on light backgrounds and White on dark backgrounds.

findings.description: Chart/data color rules, including the strict reservation of Sunshine/Gold for key figures ('strictly reserved'), are missing entirely from the candidate group despite being a distinct, strongly-worded normative statement.

payload.description: Sunshine/Gold in charts must be strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures; not to be used elsewhere in chart color scheme.

findings.description: The Coral restriction rule ('never for primary CTAs, headlines, or data' + email-section avoidance) is a distinct governance-bearing prohibition entirely absent from the candidate group; it has different polarity (must_not) and governance scope ([GENERAL] email note) than any existing rule.

payload.description: Coral is a sparing tertiary accent only and must never be used for primary CTAs, headlines, or data; general guidance to avoid Coral in email sections unless briefed.

findings.description: Source unit tags this rule with [GENERAL], indicating a governance/applicability scope facet (general brand-wide applicability) that is not reflected in the candidate rule's metadata.

The overarching hierarchy/application ratio statement (primary>secondary>tertiary dominance) governs all other color_application rules in this blob but is not represented as its own rule nor linked as a parent/governing relation to the existing rules.

payload.description: Apply palette per primary>secondary>tertiary ratio: blues and golds dominate; tertiary colors are accents/surfaces only.

findings.description: Hardness is set to strong_default with polarity must, but source unit gives no explicit imperative language ('must'), only descriptive tint assignments; verify hardness isn't overstated relative to the softer, purely descriptive phrasing used elsewhere (cf. hard_constraint wording in u_0008).

Missing relation between the strict reservation of Sunshine/Gold for chart emphasis (u_0004) and the mandated gold transparency preservation on the logo mark (u_0008) — both govern controlled/reserved use of the brand's gold asset and should be cross-referenced.
